notes: Two concurrent vibe REPL sessions launched in the SAME cwd (driver start_session step; each vibe launch mints its own session_<ts>_<hash> directory, so the two daemon session_ids are distinct regardless of shared cwd). Identity is directory-based, so the rows structurally cannot flap. Each phase anchors on a WORKING transition rather than ready, because vibe emits a pre-session proc-<PID> row born in ready that never enters working — anchoring on ready would greedily match that placeholder. Assertions: (1) session 1 does an independent working->ready turn; (2) a DISTINCT session appears (s2_birth carries new_session:true) and does its own working->ready turn; (3) session 1 is driven AGAIN while session 2 is alive (s1_turn2_*, pinned same_session_as s1_turn1_start), proving its distinct row stayed stable and did not merge into session 2. PID equality is deliberately NOT asserted — two same-cwd vibe processes have an ambiguous PID bind (caveat); session-id distinctness (directory-keyed) is the reliable isolation signal. Observations assert model (mistral-medium-3.5, from live sidecars) + tokens_nonzero (sidecar context_tokens on turn_done); cost is NOT asserted (daemon emits no per-turn cost for vibe). BACKFLOW (re-spec): mistral-vibe is a presession adapter -- birth re-modeled as presession_birth -> session_birth (new_session, different session_id) templated from the codex sibling; observations now assert agent only (cost & tokens are sidecar-only, unverifiable in the offline replay golden, like kiro-cli), superseding any model/tokens claim above.
source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Multiple sessions in same cwd

text: Session 1 proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch (PID bound before its transcript dir exists)
Session 1 real session_<ts>_<hash> reconciles (different session_id) when its transcript directory appears, born ready
Session 1 first prompt drives ready -> working
Session 1 first turn completes: working -> ready
Session 2 launches in the SAME cwd: its proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready (new_session proves it is not session 1)
Session 2 real session_<ts>_<hash> reconciles as a DISTINCT session_id/directory in the same cwd, born ready
Session 2 first prompt drives ready -> working -- independent of session 1
Session 2 first turn completes: working -> ready
Session 1 is driven AGAIN while session 2 is still alive -- a working transition on session 1's original directory-keyed id proves its row stayed distinct and did not merge into session 2
Session 1 second turn completes: working -> ready
